mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust supply-side activity, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns show negative correlation with combined INDPRO-HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in consumer spending (measured by RSAFS) during late-cycle expansion signals sustained demand momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns as earnings growth outpaces macroeconomic risks.
failure_condition: SPX underperforms baseline by > 10% in 60-day horizon during RSAFS rebounds, or RSAFS growth correlates negatively with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent disinflation (CPIAUCSL below 2% annually) sustains low-interest-rate environments, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 70%+ of instances with CPIAUCSL < 2%, or inflation spikes without corresponding equity declines.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 100k jobs in a month signals a tightening labor market, which drives wage inflation and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or payroll growth correlates negatively with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in both the federal funds rate and unemployment rate indicate accommodative monetary policy and strong labor market conditions, which jointly drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for 60-day horizon, or 60-day returns show negative correlation with combined FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trends.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in industrial production following a recessionary dip signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which supports positive S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery periods.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances with INDPRO rebounds, or industrial production declines without corresponding equity losses.
